tasks.title: Add status filtering to job listing across API, CLI, and TUI
evidence: internal/store/store.go: ListByStatus() function exists (lines 63-98) with Status parameter and pagination via ListByStatusOptions, but is not currently exposed via API/CLI/TUI.
internal/api/server.go: handleJobs() (lines 421-437) supports limit/offset query params but no status filter. Uses store.ListOpts() instead of ListByStatus().
internal/cli/cli.go: runJobs() list subcommand (lines 1002-1023) has --limit and --offset flags but no --status flag. Uses store.ListOpts().
internal/ui/tui/tui.go: fetchJobs() (lines 107-121) calls store.ListOpts() with hardcoded limit=100/offset=0, no filtering capability.
internal/model/job.go: Valid status values defined (lines 14-18): queued, running, succeeded, failed, canceled.
plan: Add status query param to /v1/jobs API endpoint that uses store.ListByStatus() when provided, fallback to ListOpts() when omitted.
Add --status flag to CLI 'spartan jobs list' command that passes status to store.ListByStatus() when specified.
Add status filter capability to TUI (keyboard trigger or persistent filter).
Add tests in internal/api/server_test.go for status-filtered listing across all interfaces.
Verify that default behavior (no status filter) returns all jobs unchanged.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Store.ListByStatus() already exists with proper implementation (SQL WHERE status = ? with pagination).
Valid status values: queued, running, succeeded, failed, canceled.
TUI filtering should align with RQ-0047 (TUI pagination/details) for consistent UX.

tasks.title: Upgrade TUI to support job details, pagination, and cancel actions
evidence: internal/ui/tui/tui.go: UI only lists jobs with ID/Kind/Status/UpdatedAt columns, supports q (quit) and r (refresh) keybindings.
internal/ui/tui/tui.go: fetchJobs() uses store.ListOpts() with hardcoded Limit=100/Offset=0 (line 109), no pagination or filtering.
internal/ui/tui/tui.go: model struct only has jobs []string and err field (lines 14-19), no selection state or detail view.
internal/ui/tui/tui.go: No test file exists (no internal/ui/tui/*_test.go files found).
internal/jobs/manager.go: CancelJob() exists (line 223) and handles both active and queued jobs via manager.activeJobs map.
internal/store/store.go: Get(id) returns full Job model with params/resultPath/error fields (line 235).
internal/exporter/exporter.go: ExportStream() supports jsonl/json/md/csv formats (line 90) for viewing/exporting results.
plan: Add selection state to TUI model (selectedJobID string) and implement cursor navigation (up/down arrows).
Add detail view pane showing full job info (ID, Kind, Status, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, Params summary, Error if any).
Add pagination controls (page up/down or Left/Right arrows) using store.ListOpts() with configurable Limit/Offset.
Add 'c' keybinding to cancel selected job via manager.CancelJob(), handle both server-managed and local-cancel scenarios.
Add 'e' keybinding to export/view results for selected job using exporter.ExportStream() with format selection.
Add 'd' keybinding to drill down into job details (full params, result path, etc.).
Implement TUI test file (internal/ui/tui/tui_test.go) with smoke test for job selection, pagination, and actions.
Update Smoke() function to verify pagination, selection, and cancel functionality work correctly.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: TUI currently only receives store, not manager - needs manager access to call CancelJob() directly.
store.Get() already supports retrieving full job details by ID (line 235 in store.go).
exporter.ExportStream() already supports streaming results to stdout or file (line 90 in exporter.go).
CLI has spartan jobs get, spartan jobs list, spartan jobs cancel commands - TUI should provide equivalent UX.
Pagination should handle edge cases (no more results, negative offset).
Detail view should truncate long Params/Error to fit TUI layout.

tasks.title: Add auth profile + login flow support to web UI
evidence: api/openapi.yaml: ScrapeRequest/CrawlRequest/ResearchRequest already include authProfile field (lines 879, 919, 963) and auth: AuthOptions (lines 882, 922, 966).
api/openapi.yaml: AuthOptions already includes loginUrl, loginUserSelector, loginPassSelector, loginSubmitSelector, loginUser, loginPass (lines 853-865).
web/src/api/types.gen.ts: Generated types already include ScrapeRequest/CrawlRequest/ResearchRequest with authProfile and auth fields (lines 159-198).
web/src/App.tsx: buildAuth() function (lines 1094-1109) only constructs basic/headers/cookies/query; does not include login flow fields (loginUrl, loginUserSelector, etc.).
web/src/App.tsx: Request payloads in submitScrape/submitCrawl/submitResearch (lines 227-240, 261-276, 297-312) use buildAuth() but never set authProfile field.
web/src/App.tsx: UI state includes authBasic/headersRaw/cookiesRaw/queryRaw (lines 87-90) but no authProfile state or login flow state.
internal/cli/cli.go: CLI already supports --auth-profile flag and login flow flags (--login-url, --login-user-selector, --login-pass-selector, --login-submit-selector).
plan: Add authProfile state to App.tsx and fetch auth profiles from /v1/auth/profiles API to populate a dropdown selector.
Add login flow state fields (loginUrl, loginUserSelector, loginPassSelector, loginSubmitSelector, loginUser, loginPass) to App.tsx.
Update buildAuth() function to include login flow fields (loginUrl, loginUserSelector, loginPassSelector, loginSubmitSelector, loginUser, loginPass) in the returned AuthOptions object.
Add authProfile field to request payloads in submitScrape(), submitCrawl(), and submitResearch().
Add UI controls for login flow fields (optional, collapsed or separate section since not always needed).
Import getV1AuthProfiles from web/src/api/index.ts and call it to load available profiles.
Add tests in web/src/App.test.tsx to verify auth payloads include authProfile and login flow fields when specified.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: API types are already up-to-date; no need to regenerate (api/openapi.yaml already defines all required fields).
The backend API already supports authProfile and login flow; this is purely a web UI parity task.
Login flow fields should be optional and only included in the payload if set to avoid cluttering requests for simple auth scenarios.
Consider adding a 'Use Auth Profile' toggle vs 'Custom Auth' switch for cleaner UX.

tasks.title: Expose pipeline processors and incremental toggle in web UI
evidence: web/src/App.tsx:227-240: submitScrape() payload includes url/headless/playwright/timeout/auth/extract but NO pipeline or incremental field.
web/src/App.tsx:261-276: submitCrawl() payload includes url/maxDepth/maxPages/headless/playwright/timeout/auth/extract but NO pipeline or incremental field.
web/src/App.tsx:297-312: submitResearch() payload includes query/urls/maxDepth/maxPages/headless/playwright/timeout/auth/extract but NO pipeline or incremental field.

web/src/App.tsx:79-106: App state includes headless/usePlaywright/maxDepth/maxPages/timeout/authBasic/headersRaw/cookiesRaw/queryRaw/extractTemplate/extractValidate but NO pipeline or incremental state.
internal/cli/cli.go:124-128: CLI builds pipeline.Options with PreProcessors/PostProcessors/Transformers from flags.
internal/cli/cli.go:145: CLI passes incremental bool to CreateScrapeJob.
internal/cli/cli.go:1559-1561: CLI defines --pre-processor/--post-processor/--transformer flags (repeatable string arrays).
internal/cli/cli.go:1555: CLI defines --incremental boolean flag.
api/openapi.yaml:616-630: PipelineOptions schema defines preProcessors/postProcessors/transformers as string arrays.
api/openapi.yaml:885-886,925-926,969-970: ScrapeRequest/CrawlRequest/ResearchRequest all include optional pipeline field referencing PipelineOptions.
api/openapi.yaml:892,932,976: ScrapeRequest/CrawlRequest/ResearchRequest all include optional incremental boolean field.
web/src/api/types.gen.ts:18-22: Generated PipelineOptions type includes preProcessors/postProcessors/transformers string arrays.
web/src/api/types.gen.ts:159-198: Generated ScrapeRequest/CrawlRequest/ResearchRequest all include optional pipeline and incremental fields.
plan: Add state variables to App.tsx for pipeline options: preProcessors (string), postProcessors (string), transformers (string), incremental (boolean).
Add UI section for pipeline options with text inputs for preProcessors/postProcessors/transformers (comma-separated values) and checkbox for incremental.
Parse pipeline text inputs into arrays (split by comma, trim whitespace) to match PipelineOptions type structure.
Update submitScrape() to include pipeline: {preProcessors, postProcessors, transformers} and incremental in the request body.
Update submitCrawl() to include pipeline: {preProcessors, postProcessors, transformers} and incremental in the request body.
Update submitResearch() to include pipeline: {preProcessors, postProcessors, transformers} and incremental in the request body.
Add tests in web/src/App.test.tsx to verify that pipeline options are correctly parsed and included in request payloads.
Add tests in web/src/App.test.tsx to verify that incremental checkbox value is correctly included in request payloads.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: API types (web/src/api/types.gen.ts) already include PipelineOptions and incremental fields - no make generate needed.
Backend API and CLI already support pipeline and incremental options - this is purely a web UI parity task.
Pipeline options (preProcessors, postProcessors, transformers) are repeatable arrays in CLI; web UI should use comma-separated text inputs for simplicity.
incremental is a boolean toggle in CLI; web UI should use checkbox.
All three request types (scrape/crawl/research) need the same pipeline and incremental fields for consistency.

tasks.title: Make web UI results loading scalable for large JSONL outputs
evidence: web/src/App.tsx:360-361: loadResults() uses response.text() and splits all lines, loading full results into memory.
web/src/App.tsx:364-371: parseItems loops through all lines and parses every JSON object into memory.
internal/api/server.go:524-530: /v1/jobs/{id}/results for jsonl format uses http.ServeFile() with no pagination.
internal/api/server.go:534-555: For json/md/csv formats, uses exporter.ExportStream() but still processes entire file.
api/openapi.yaml:459-508: /v1/jobs/{id}/results endpoint only has format parameter, no limit/offset pagination.
internal/exporter/exporter.go:404-417: exportJSONLStream streams input to output but cannot be used for pagination.
internal/exporter/exporter.go:534-553: parseLinesReader() loads all items into memory with no limit/offset support.
Large crawl results (1000+ pages) cause web UI to freeze or crash due to memory exhaustion.
plan: Add limit and offset query parameters to /v1/jobs/{id}/results endpoint in api/openapi.yaml for paginated JSONL responses.
Add Limit() and Offset() helper functions to internal/exporter/exporter.go to parse query parameters with defaults (limit=100, offset=0).
Add ExportPaginated() function to internal/exporter/exporter.go that returns a slice of items with offset/limit applied.
Update internal/api/server.go handleJobResults() to call ExportPaginated() for jsonl format when limit/offset params present.
Regenerate TS client with make generate to include new API parameters.
Update web/src/App.tsx loadResults() to track current page (page state variable) and request limit/offset from API.
Add pagination controls to web UI (Previous/Next buttons, page number display, jump to page input) in results view.
Update web/src/App.tsx to clear result items when format changes or new job is selected to avoid stale state.
Add tests in web/src/App.test.tsx for pagination (verify page changes, correct data per page, edge cases).
Add tests in internal/api/server_test.go for limit/offset query parameter handling.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Default page size of 100 items balances UI responsiveness with API load.
offset is 0-indexed: page=1 means offset=0, page=2 means offset=100, etc.
Client-side lazy loading is insufficient; must paginate at API level to avoid transferring huge payloads.
For formats other than jsonl (json/md/csv), pagination is less critical since these are typically single-item exports or small reports.
Consider adding X-Total-Count response header for total item count to enable 'Showing X-Y of Z results' display.

tasks.title: Reject unknown JSON fields in API requests for clearer UX
evidence: internal/api/server.go:210: handleScrape uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields, silently ignoring typos like 'ur' instead of 'url'.
internal/api/server.go:281: handleCrawl uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server.go:354: handleResearch uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server.go:585: handleAuthProfile (PUT) uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&profile) without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server.go:629,657,699: handleAuthImport/handleAuthExport/handleSchedules use json.NewDecoder without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server_test.go: No tests exist for unknown field validation; existing tests focus on missing fields, invalid values, and malformed JSON.
plan: Add decoder := json.NewDecoder(r.Body); decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() pattern to handleScrape at line 210.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleCrawl at line 281.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleResearch at line 354.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleAuthProfile (PUT) at line 585.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleAuthImport, handleAuthExport, handleSchedules at lines 629,657,699.
Add test in internal/api/server_test.go: TestUnknownFieldsRejected with subtests for /v1/scrape, /v1/crawl, /v1/research, /v1/schedules, and /v1/auth/profiles endpoints.
Verify that unknown fields return 400 with clear error message containing 'unknown field' or 'json: unknown field'.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Go json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() returns json.UnmarshalTypeError for unknown fields, which should trigger the existing error handling at line 211 (handleScrape), 282 (handleCrawl), 355 (handleResearch), etc.
The writeJSONError helper already returns 400 status for 'invalid json' errors, so DisallowUnknownFields errors will be handled correctly without code changes.
Only applies to POST/PUT endpoints with request bodies: handleScrape, handleCrawl, handleResearch, handleSchedules (POST), handleAuthProfile (PUT), handleAuthImport, handleAuthExport.
